title: NIS2 Cooperation Group and Sector Information Sharing Participation
exclusions: Does not judge lawfulness of shared data, NDAs, or classification decisions; does not replace formal ENISA or national cooperation filings.
evidence: Saved search `UC-22.2.44` results, associated `snow:task` references, summarized `stix` volume stats, and archived dashboard PDF in `index=audit_evidence`.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Art.14 is assigned contributing assurance because this UC provides concrete Splunk evidence for the observable control condition, while policy approval, legal interpretation, and official filings remain outside Splunk.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIS2 Art.14 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.2.44: NIS2 Cooperation Group and Sector Information Sharing Participation.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.2.44' running on nis2_cooperation_group_register.csv, index=itsm (meeting tasks), STIX/TAXII ingest metrics (index=threat_intel), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Simulate a forum with `participation_gap=1` by closing the ServiceNow task in `Resolved` instead of `Closed` and confirm the search lists the forum; separately zero-out STIX events for a feed and verify the low `indicators_consumed` branch fires when under the configured floor.
controlTest.negativeScenario: When the latest task is `Closed` within cadence and thirty-day `stix` volume exceeds the forum-specific floor, the dashboard stays empty for that `group_id`, showing the negative path for healthy participation.

dataSources: `nis2_cooperation_group_register.csv`; `index=itsm` tasks (`sourcetype=snow:task` or `snow:sn_grc_policy`) with `assignment_group`, `state`, `closed_at`; `index=threat_intel` `sourcetype=stix` events carrying `indicator_id`, `feed_name`; Splunk Enterprise Security ([Splunkbase 263](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263)) for intel pipeline health; Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow ([Splunkbase 1928](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1928)).
app: Splunk Enterprise Security ([Splunkbase 263](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263)); Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow ([Splunkbase 1928](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1928)); HTTP Event Collector for supplemental STIX feeds.
spl:
| inputlookup nis2_cooperation_group_register.csv
| lookup nis2_isac_kfp_thresholds group_id OUTPUT feed_floor_threshold exception_until
| eval feed_floor_threshold=coalesce(feed_floor_threshold,1000)
| join group_id type=left [
    search index=itsm sourcetype=snow:task (short_description="*ISAC*" OR short_description="*cooperation group*") earliest=-180d
    | stats latest(state) as last_meeting_state latest(closed_at) as last_meeting_date by assignment_group
    | rename assignment_group as group_id
]
| eval participation_gap=if(last_meeting_state!="Closed" OR isnull(last_meeting_date),1,0)
| join group_id type=left [
    search index=threat_intel sourcetype=stix earliest=-30d
    | stats dc(indicator_id) as indicators_consumed by feed_name
    | rename feed_name as group_id
]
| eval indicators_consumed=coalesce(indicators_consumed,0)
| where participation_gap=1 OR indicators_consumed < feed_floor_threshold
| where isnull(exception_until) OR now()>strptime(exception_until,"%Y-%m-%d")
| table group_id, last_meeting_state, last_meeting_date, indicators_consumed, participation_gap, feed_floor_threshold
description: Correlates the cooperation-forum register with closed ServiceNow meeting tasks and STIX indicator volume per named feed so lapses in sector ISAC participation or silent intel pipelines become visible.
value: Public affairs and cyber leaders can demonstrate active collaboration and functioning information exchanges without maintaining separate spreadsheets, which matters when policymakers ask for proof of collective resilience investments.
implementation: (1) Maintain `nis2_cooperation_group_register.csv` with `group_id` aligned to ServiceNow `assignment_group` and intel `feed_name`. (2) Ingest STIX via ES TAXII or HEC. (3) Tune `feed_floor` per forum. (4) Monthly CISO review. (5) Store exports in `index=audit_evidence`.
visualization: Participation gap table, timeline of meetings, single value active STIX feeds.
knownFalsePositives:
• **Summer holiday quorum gaps** — Distinguish: meeting task still `Work in Progress` but charter allows August pause. Suppress: `lookup nis2_isac_kfp_thresholds` lowering `feed_floor` or pausing alerts for 30 days.
• **Intel feed renamed in TAXII server** — Distinguish: `feed_name` mismatch yields zero `indicators_consumed`. Suppress: update register CSV; time-bound exception register entry.
• **Confidential side channel minutes** — Distinguish: official attendance kept outside ServiceNow. Suppress: manual attestation flag `alt_evidence=true` on register row with review ticket.
• **Synthetic test STIX drop** — Distinguish: purple-team feed spikes then goes quiet. Suppress: filter `where feed_name!="purple_team_qa"` or use exception lookup.
equipmentModels: Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928, task and incident tables for meeting evidence)
Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud (HEC for supplemental STIX JSON from national CSIRTs)
Splunk Enterprise Security 7.x (Splunkbase 263, Intelligence Downloads for TAXII/STIX feeds)
detailedImplementation:
## Prerequisites

• **Splunk**: Enterprise ≥9.2 or Splunk Cloud (current). Scheduling requires a search head not under memory pressure (check `index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd component=ProcessRunner` for OOM warnings before deploying a new scheduled search).
• **Technology Add-on**: Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase app ID 1928) must be installed on search heads and indexers. Verify with `| rest /services/apps/local | search label="Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow" | table label version`.
• **Indexes**: `itsm`, `threat_intel` must exist on all indexers with retention matching the regulation's minimum (PCI: 1y, HIPAA: 6y, NIST 800-53: 1-3y per FIPS 199, SOX: 7y). Set `frozenTimePeriodInSecs` accordingly in `indexes.conf`.
• **Lookups**: `nis2_isac_kfp_thresholds`, `nis2_cooperation_group_register.csv` must be deployed to the search-tier app and refreshed on a documented schedule. For KV Store lookups, verify with `| rest /servicesNS/-/-/data/transforms/lookups | search name IN ('nis2_isac_kfp_thresholds','nis2_cooperation_group_register.csv')`.
• **Regulatory mapping**: This detection evidences clause `Art.14`. The control objective is: "Evidence that NIS2 Art.14 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.2.44: NIS2 Cooperation Group and Sector Information Sharing Participation." — every alert payload should include this text so downstream consumers know the compliance purpose.

## Step 1 — Configure data collection

Data collection is performed by the Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase app ID 1928), landing events in `itsm`, `threat_intel` under `snow:task`, `stix`. REST-polled ticket tables produce 500-5000 records per sync per table; at 300s interval expect ~3-8 GB/month for a mid-size service desk.

Configure the input through Settings > Data Inputs > ServiceNow Service Account. A reference `inputs.conf` stanza looks like:

```ini
[snow://{instance_slug}]
url = https://<instance>.service-now.com
table = {table_name}
interval = 300
index = itsm
sourcetype = {sourcetype}
```

The following fields are authoritative for this detection once the TA parses them: `number, state, priority, assignment_group, opened_at, closed_at, sys_updated_on, caller_id, category, subcategory`. Verify they are extracted at search time with `index=itsm earliest=-15m | fieldsummary` before scheduling the detection.


5 operational considerations apply to the rollout of this detection:


• **Hint 1:** Maintain `nis2_cooperation_group_register.csv` with `group_id` aligned to ServiceNow `assignment_group` and intel `feed_name`. The lookup table identified here is a long-lived compliance artifact; version it in source control and track every change with a pull-request review.

• **Hint 2:** Ingest STIX via ES TAXII or HEC. Ingestion SLA drift is usually invisible until it breaks something — monitor the feed's freshness with a companion search that alerts when the newest event is older than the expected cadence.

• **Hint 3:** Tune `feed_floor` per forum. Exclusions should be time-bound and logged — a permanent exclusion baked into the search query becomes invisible over time and future owners cannot tell it was intentional.

• **Hint 4:** Monthly CISO review. This is an operational consideration that influences either the detection's accuracy or its maintainability — document the approach chosen in the UC runbook.

• **Hint 5:** Store exports in `index=audit_evidence`. Report-generation cadence must match the regulatory reporting cycle; a monthly digest satisfies an annual review requirement but not a quarterly one.

## Step 2 — Implement the search

For "NIS2 Cooperation Group and Sector Information Sharing Participation," the aggregation produces the result shape required by the NIS2 control — each row is a summary record the regulation expects to be presentable at audit time.

This search begins with `| inputlookup nis2_cooperation_group_register.csv` — the base dataset is a CSV/KV Store lookup, not live event data. This inverts the usual Splunk assumption: the detection does not stream from an index, it iterates a register. The register itself is the authoritative source of truth (contracts, policies, sign-offs, attestations) and the search joins it against indexed events to find gaps.

Lookup authority: treat `nis2_cooperation_group_register.csv` as a primary compliance artifact. Its contents ARE the answer the regulator will ask for ('show me your vendor list,' 'show me your CDE inventory,' 'show me your policy exceptions'). Implement the following controls on the lookup itself, not just the search:

• Version the CSV in a git repository with a mandatory pull-request review for every change.
• Maintain a signed-record trail: who changed what, when, and with what approval. A lookup with a silent edit history is an audit liability.
• Automate a nightly diff: `| inputlookup nis2_cooperation_group_register.csv | eval hash=md5(...) | outputlookup append=true nis2_cooperation_group_register_audit.csv` — this produces an immutable record of the register's state over time.
• Lock the lookup with role-based access: only the content owner has `list_inputs` + `write_file` on the path; everyone else is read-only.

The live-data side of the join (across the last 180 days) is the detection trigger — when the register and the telemetry disagree, that's a finding.

**Schedule configuration** (`savedsearches.conf`):
```ini
[UC-22.2.44: NIS2 Cooperation Group and Sector Information Sharing Partic]
cron_schedule = 0 */6 * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -180d
dispatch.latest_time = now
actions = summary_index
action.summary_index._name = summary_compliance
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.2.44,reg=NIS2
```

## Step 3 — Validate deployment

**(a) Field extraction check:** Run the following to confirm the TA is extracting every field the detection relies on:

```spl
index=itsm sourcetype=snow:task earliest=-1h
| fieldsummary
| where count=0
| table field
```

Any row returned is a field the TA claims to extract but isn't appearing in recent data — it's either a rename upstream, a TA version mismatch, or a data-format change.

**(b) Required-field presence:** This detection specifically needs: `group_id`, `indicators_consumed`, `last_meeting_date`, `last_meeting_state`, `participation_gap`. Run:

```spl
index=itsm sourcetype=snow:task earliest=-1h
| head 100
| table group_id, indicators_consumed, last_meeting_date, last_meeting_state, participation_gap
```

Every row should have non-null values for the fields the detection's `where` clause filters on. Nulls cause silent drops in the result set.

**(c) Lookup freshness:** The detection joins against `nis2_isac_kfp_thresholds`. Check its modification time and row count:

```spl
| inputlookup nis2_isac_kfp_thresholds
| eval _lookup_row=1
| stats count as rows
```

Compare the row count to the expected baseline; an unexpected drop is a sign the upstream export truncated.

**(e) Negative test:** Generate a synthetic 'should not alert' event using the Splunk Sample Generator or a crafted `| makeresults` event and confirm the detection does NOT fire on it. A detection that fires on everything is equivalent to a detection that fires on nothing.

## Step 4 — Operationalize (dashboard and reporting)

Create a companion dashboard titled `UC-22.2.44: NIS2 evidence` with the following panel layout:

**Panel 1 — Participation Gap Table (the key fields):** Configure according to standard Splunk visualization patterns for this panel type.

**Panel 2 — Timeline Of Meetings (the key fields):** Configure according to standard Splunk visualization patterns for this panel type.

**Panel 3 — Single Value Active Stix Feeds (the key fields):** Configure according to standard Splunk visualization patterns for this panel type.


Time picker defaults: set to last 30 days with named presets for 24h, 7d, 30d, 90d, and 1y — the auditor needs all five windows without retyping. Enable scheduled PDF export for the full dashboard on the regulation's reporting cadence.

## Step 5 — Troubleshooting and regulation-specific caveats

**Search returns no results when violations are expected:** Verify time-window alignment, index existence, and field extractions in that order. Most 'missing results' are simpler configuration issues before they're detection-logic issues.

**Search times out:** Check the search job inspector (`Activity > Jobs > <this UC's last run> > Inspect`). The phase consuming the time (fetch, command, summary) indicates whether the issue is data-layer (too wide a window, unaccelerated datamodel), command-layer (expensive `transaction` or `eventstats`), or post-aggregation.


**NIS2-specific failure modes:**

• 24/7 reporting obligation (Art. 23): NIS2 requires early warning within 24 hours of awareness of a significant incident. Detections that back this SLA must compute elapsed time from the SOC's first-acknowledged timestamp (not the resolve-time) and must trigger independently of business hours — the regulatory clock does not pause overnight.

• Member-state variation: NIS2 is transposed individually by each EU Member State with some latitude on thresholds and sector scoping. A detection correct for German IT-SiG 3 (BSI) may be misaligned with Dutch CWV or French LPM. Use `nis2_member_state_config.csv` to parametrize thresholds per jurisdiction; never hardcode.

• Essential vs Important entity criteria: the applicable obligations depend on sector + size. Before alerting, verify the monitored entity's designation; a detection firing on an out-of-scope subsidiary causes unnecessary regulatory notifications that erode trust with the national CSIRT.

• Supply-chain (Art. 21(2)(d)) extent: NIS2 explicitly includes direct suppliers' cybersecurity posture. Detections must cover monitored-by-you-not-owned-by-you infrastructure (outsourced SaaS, managed SIEM tenants) — these are in scope even if operationally run by a third party. BA/BAA-style governance applies.
grandmaExplanation: We watch both your meeting follow-through for industry cooperation groups and whether fresh safety tips are still flowing in from trusted partners. If meetings stall or the tip line goes quiet, we flag it so leaders can nudge the right people.
